blocks.text: DTC P0134: LAF sensor activity abnormal

The LAF sensor activates the catalytic action of the sensor element by heating the sensor element with a heater and keeping it at a constant high temperature in order to calculate the accurate air-fuel ratio. The PGM-FI ECU monitors the condition of the LAF sensor by monitoring its internal resistance value.

items.text: If the LAF sensor is not activated within a set time after the LAF sensor heater is energized, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the LAF sensor heater has failed and stores the DTC.

The LAF sensor heater is turned on and off within a set time period, and the state of the LAF sensor heater is detected by monitoring the LAF sensor internal resistance value. The PGM-FI ECU judges that the LAF sensor heater is faulty and stores the DTC.

blocks.text: Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

Start the engine and leave the engine idling for at least 1 minute. If the engine coolant temperature [water temperature sensor] is below -8°C when the engine is started, leave the engine idling for at least 4 minutes.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
